rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING, LLC VS. VERONICA PETERKIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 1, CROSS-DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s ("Chase") demurrer to cross-complainant Stork's (aka Peterkin) cross-complaint is sustained and overruled in part. (The Court's full tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Chase is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LVOVICH & SZUCSKO, P.C. A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL VS. YURI GALASHOV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LVOVICH & SZUCSKO, P.C.'s Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and is therefore granted. Defendant Galashov shall respond to plaintiff's Second Set of Requests for Production and First Set of Special Interrogatories and shall produce all requested documents, all without objection, within 15 days of the date of signature of this Order. In addition, Defendant Galashov is ordered to pay sanctions for discovery misuse in the amount of $2161.00 to counsel to moving party within 15 days of the date of signature of this Order. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC WINE DISTRIBUTORS, INC. VS. ELAINE HEATHER HARTSHORN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (1) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents From Gino Pacella, And (2) Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 2, (part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) DEFENDANTs ELAINE HARTSHORN, SIDEKICK DELIVERY, INC., and J.F. HILLEBRAND USA, INC.'s (1) Motion To Compel Documents From Gino Pacella, And (2) Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel production is granted to the extent hereinafter set forth. Plaintiff's general objections are overruled. Requests concerning the business operations and history of plaintiff's Northern California facility are relevant to the subject matter and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Events before and after the duration of the Sidekick contract are relevant to the subject matter and reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence as to plaintiff's liability and damage claims. Mr. Pacella appeared as an individual witness and not as a corporate representative. Mr. Pacella has failed to submit any opposition or sworn explanation of his efforts to respond to the Notice of Taking Deposition. Further, Mr. Pacella admittedly made only a partial and insufficient search of the records under his possession or control and produced only secondary records instead of source documents and electronically stored records. Plaintiff's unsworn assertions as to Mr. Pacella's attempts to comply with the Notice of Taking deposition and to produce documents are unpersuasive. The distinction between an individual witness like Mr. Pacella and a designated corporate representative negates defendants' appeals to a supposed duty to find answers to examiner's questions, except as to his personal knowledge and documents now or previously in his possession. Maldonado v. Superior Court, 94 Cal. App. 4th 1390 (2002), on which defendants rely, makes clear that the law imposes a duty to make inquiries and find answers on a designated corporate representative but not on a corporate officer who may often engage in buck passing or denials of knowledge with impunity. Accordingly, Mr. Pacella is ordered as follows with reference to Requests Nos. 3-4, 5-6, 9-10, 16, 24-25, and 34-42: (1) to appear for a further deposition upon reasonable notice from plaintiff's counsel; (2) to conduct a thorough search of the documents and records within his possession or control; (3) to produce all responsive documents and records, including source documents and electronically stored information, including without limitation emails and QuickBooks files; and (4) to answer all proper questions as to his knowledge of relevant matters and of the records now or previously within his possession or control. (tentative ruling continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL UMANA PORTILLO ET AL VS. ARIEL RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bci Coca-Cola Bottling Company Of Los Angeles' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff Claudia Portillo And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 3, Defendant Bci Coca-Cola Bottling Company Of Los Angeles' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff Claudia Portillo And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motions to compel deposition are unopposed and are therefore granted. Plaintiffs Manual Portilla and Claudia Portilla shall appear for their respective depositions on a date or dates to be set by written notice from counsel to moving party. The Court finds that the award of sanctions would be unjust under the circumstances of this case. However, plaintiffs are cautioned that the failure to obey this order to provide discovery may constitute a misuse of the discovery process which may be punished by an (1) an award of the expenses incurred by the opposing party; (2) an order designating facts as established in accordance with the claim of the opposing party; (3) an order prohibiting you from introducing designated matters in evidence; (4) an order terminating part or all of your action, striking your pleading and entering judgment against you by default; and/or (5) treating the misuse of the discovery process as contempt of court. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ETHEL BARAJAS ET AL VS. PRASAD G. KILARU MD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 7, DEFENDANT WASHINGTON TOWNSHIP HEALTH CARE DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Plaintiffs' counsel's motion to be relieved as counsel was granted by order dated July 20, 2020. However, that order does not become effective until the filing of the proof of service of the order on the clients, which has not yet been filed. The demurrer was not served on plaintiffs, and it is unclear whether they were represented by counsel on July 28, when their opposition to the demurrer was due. Accordingly, defendants must re-notice the demurrer and serve it on plaintiffs once the status of their representation is clarified. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION VS. 3D PRINT OF NORTH AMERICA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Makerbot Industries, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Makerbot Industries, LLC. Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment as to defendant BASF Corporation is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order and consent judgment and must email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PIRRAGLIA VS. CHINA LIVE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Hopmonk Holdings Llc Request For Sanctions Against China Live Llc And Shane Anderies Esq
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2020, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PIRRAGLIA's Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Hopmonk Holdings LLC Request For Sanctions Against China Live LLC And Shane Anderies Esq Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to quash subpoena is denied. Plaintiff's privacy interest in his former employer's records is outweighed by defendant's need for the information and the public interest in the discovery of the truth of matters in litigation. In particular, the discovery sought may produce evidence of resume fraud, misrepresentation or other misconduct, character evidence and/or impeachment evidence. The Court will issue a protective order to protect plaintiff's privacy interest as against any minimal invasion as a result of this order. Counsel are ordered to meet and confer as to the terms of the protective order and shall submit the proposed protective order in a single document in Word format within seven days of the date of this order. Any disputes as to the language or terms of the protective order shall be highlighted in the proposal. The Court finds that the award of sanctions would be unjust under the circumstances. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
